name: Three Sum
description: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets. Return the triplets in any order, but each triplet should be sorted in non-decreasing order.
tests:
def test_basic():
    result = three_sum([-1, 0, 1, 2, -1, -4])
    result = [sorted(t) for t in result]
    result.sort()
    assert result == [[-1, -1, 2], [-1, 0, 1]]

def test_duplicates_handled():
    result = three_sum([-2, 0, 1, 1, 2])
    result = [sorted(t) for t in result]
    result.sort()
    assert result == [[-2, 0, 2], [-2, 1, 1]]

def test_large_set():
    result = three_sum([-1, 0, 1, 2, -1, -4, -2, -3, 3, 0, 4])
    result = [sorted(t) for t in result]
    result.sort()
    assert [-4, 0, 4] in result
    assert [-4, 1, 3] in result
    assert [-1, 0, 1] in result
constraints: Solution set must not contain duplicate triplets
Each triplet should be sorted in non-decreasing order
